text.title: django-quality-linting Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-quality-linting as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using # type: ignore without a reason comment, or scattering ignore comments everywhere because the stubs are incomplete.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unexplained ignores accumulate and hide real errors. For DRF serializers (where stubs are incomplete), install djangorestframework-stubs and document the specific limitation in the comment.

failure-mode.description: Running mypy repo-wide with --strict from day one on an existing codebase.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hundreds of errors appear immediately and developers disable mypy entirely. Enable strict per-module in [tool.mypy.overrides] incrementally, allowing the team to fix one module at a time.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The plugin cannot resolve settings-dependent types (INSTALLED_APPS, AUTH_USER_MODEL) without the settings module path. Results in spurious errors across the entire ORM layer.

failure-mode.description: Adding T20 (no-print) rule to ruff but leaving print() calls in management commands and scripts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Management commands legitimately use self.stdout.write(), not print(). Add T20 per-file-ignore for management/commands/ or convert to self.stdout.write().

failure-mode.description: Running MegaLinter or full linting suite as a pre-commit hook instead of CI-only.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Heavy containers add 30+ seconds per commit; developers bypass with --no-verify and the local gate dies. Native tools (ruff, mypy) complete in under 1 second per commit.
